description: Unique transaction identifier with TXN_ prefix
Unique customer identifier with CUST_ prefix
Transaction date and time (all transactions within 2023)
Transaction amount in Nigerian Naira. Legitimate amounts follow channel-specific log-normal distributions. Fraud amounts are adjusted to match NIBSS channel-specific average loss patterns
Transaction channel. Distribution calibrated to NIBSS 2023 data
example_values: Mobile, Web, POS, IB, ECOM, ATM

description: Merchant category classification
example_values: Retail, Grocery, Fuel, ATM_Withdrawal, Transfer, Bill_Payment, Airtime, Restaurant, Transport, Medical, Education, Entertainment, Fashion, Electronics

description: Nigerian bank processing the transaction
example_values: GTBank, FirstBank, Zenith, UBA, Access, Fidelity, Sterling, FCMB, Wema, Union

description: Nigerian state where transaction originated. Lagos-centric distribution (48%)
example_values: Lagos, Abuja, Rivers, Ogun, Oyo, Other

description: Customer age band based on NIBSS demographic data
Hour of transaction extracted from timestamp (0-23)
Day of week extracted from timestamp (0=Monday through 6=Sunday)
Month of transaction extracted from timestamp (1-12)
Whether the transaction occurred on Saturday or Sunday
Whether the transaction occurred during Nigerian banking peak hours (10-11 AM and 2-4 PM)
Number of transactions by the same customer in the preceding 24-hour window
Sum of transaction amounts by the same customer in the preceding 24-hour window
Mean transaction amount by the same customer over the preceding 7-day window
Standard deviation of transaction amounts by the same customer over the preceding 7-day window. Zero if only one transaction in window
Total number of transactions by the customer across the entire dataset
Mean transaction amount by the customer across the entire dataset
Standard deviation of transaction amounts by the customer across the entire dataset. Zero if customer has only one transaction
Number of unique transaction channels used by the customer (1-6)
Number of unique locations (states) the customer transacted from
Ratio of current transaction amount to the customer's historical mean amount. Values above 1.0 indicate above-average spending
Proportion of the customer's transactions conducted via online channels (Mobile + Web + ECOM)
Fraud label. 0 = legitimate transaction; 1 = fraudulent transaction. Overall fraud rate is 0.30% (3000 out of 1000000)
Fraud technique for fraudulent transactions. Empty for legitimate transactions
example_values: SOCIAL_ENGINEERING, ROBBERY, CARD_THEFT, PIN_COMPROMISE, PHISHING, OTHER

description: Sine component of cyclic hour encoding: sin(2*pi*hour/24). Captures cyclical time patterns for ML models
Cosine component of cyclic hour encoding: cos(2*pi*hour/24). Paired with hour_sin for full cyclical representation
Sine component of cyclic day-of-week encoding: sin(2*pi*day_of_week/7)
Cosine component of cyclic day-of-week encoding: cos(2*pi*day_of_week/7)
Sine component of cyclic month encoding: sin(2*pi*month/12)
Cosine component of cyclic month encoding: cos(2*pi*month/12)
Log-transformed transaction amount: log(1 + amount). Reduces skewness for ML models
Whether the transaction amount is a round number (amount modulo 1000 equals 0). 0 = not round; 1 = round
Transaction velocity indicator: tx_count_24h * amount_sum_24h / (amount_mean_total + 1). Higher values suggest unusual transaction bursts

Risk score assigned to the merchant category (0 to 1). High-risk categories (Electronics/Fashion/Entertainment) sampled from Beta(6;4); low-risk (Grocery/Fuel/Medical) from Beta(2;8); others from Beta(3;6)
Weighted composite risk score combining: merchant_risk_score (0.3) + normalized amount_vs_mean_ratio (0.3) + normalized velocity_score (0.2) + high_frequency_indicator (0.2, where tx_count_24h > 5)
